jobs.name: Rolling OS Docs
jobs.description: Nightly analysis of GoatOS changes — updates OS documentation in Brain module

payload.message:
You are running a scheduled overnight documentation task for GoatOS.

1. Read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/brain/os-docs/latest.md (current docs)
2. Read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/index.html and /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/api.mjs to understand current state
3. Read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/build-logs/logs.json for recent build history
4. Read /home/ubuntu/USER.md and /home/ubuntu/IDENTITY.md for context
5. Rewrite /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/brain/os-docs/latest.md with a complete, updated documentation of the entire OS: architecture, every module, every tab, every feature, tech stack, design system, cron jobs, and any recent changes.
6. Make sure the documentation is thorough but readable. Include the current date.
7. Do NOT modify any other files. Documentation only.

jobs.name: Bug Check
jobs.description: Nightly bug scan of GoatOS codebase — fix issues and log results

payload.message:
You are running a scheduled overnight bug check for GoatOS.

PRIORITY: Speed. You have 15 minutes. Be efficient.

1. Test the API first — curl each endpoint and check for errors:
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/sessions
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/briefs
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/os-docs
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/prototypes
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/overnight-builds
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/build-logs
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/ideas
   - http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/cron

2. If any endpoint fails, investigate and fix the API (api.mjs)

3. Scan /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/index.html for: broken template literals, unclosed tags, missing function references, undefined variables. Use grep to search — do NOT read the full file line by line.

4. Read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/DESIGN.md and check for obvious design rule violations.

5. Fix any bugs directly in the source files.

6. Log results: read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/build-logs/logs.json, append a new entry with id (bug-check-YYYY-MM-DD), name 'Bug Check', status (success/failed), timestamp (ISO), summary, and sections: 'API Health', 'Bugs Found', 'Fixes Applied', 'Files Modified'. If clean, log it as a clean scan.

7. Write the updated logs.json back.

RULES:
- Do NOT break working features
- Be fast — scan smartly, don't read every line
- If the API server isn't running, restart it: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os && nohup node api.mjs > /tmp/goat-os-api.log 2>&1 &

jobs.name: Overnight Self-Improvement
jobs.description: Audits one GoatOS module and builds one improvement overnight

payload.message:
You are Goat, running a scheduled overnight self-improvement task for GoatOS.

You have 15 minutes. Be focused and efficient.

== STEP 1: LOAD CONTEXT ==
Read these files:
- /home/ubuntu/USER.md (Albs — Chief of Staff at Hampr, what he cares about)
- /home/ubuntu/IDENTITY.md (you are Goat, executive assistant)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/DESIGN.md (design rules: no emojis in the OS, Lucide icons, Ubuntu fonts, module colours)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/PURPOSE.md (lab vision)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/self-improvement/ideas.json (backlog)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/build-logs/logs.json (what was already built — READ THIS CAREFULLY)

== STEP 2: PICK TONIGHT'S MODULE ==
Rotate modules in order: Ops -> Brain -> Lab -> Ops -> ...
Check the last 3 build log entries to see which module was last improved. Pick the NEXT one in rotation.

MODULE OWNERSHIP (never cross these boundaries):
- OPS: System health, monitoring, session management, cron health, org chart, infrastructure dashboards
- BRAIN: Daily briefs, automations, OS docs, memory, intelligence, data analysis
- LAB: Prototypes, build logs, self-improvement backlog, experiments

== STEP 3: DEDUP CHECK ==
Before building ANYTHING:
1. grep the codebase for similar functionality: grep -r 'keyword' /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/index.html
2. Read the build logs to see what was already shipped
3. If something similar exists, IMPROVE it instead of rebuilding
4. If the feature already exists and works well, pick a DIFFERENT improvement

== STEP 4: BUILD ==
1. Identify ONE high-value improvement for tonight's module
2. It must align with Albs' goals: efficiency, delegation, Hampr ROI, GoatOS vision
3. Implement directly in the source files
4. Test — curl API endpoints to verify nothing broke
5. Make sure the feature lives in the CORRECT module (not scattered across modules)

== STEP 5: LOG ==
1. Update /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/self-improvement/ideas.json
2. Read /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/build-logs/logs.json
3. Append a new entry: id (self-improve-YYYY-MM-DD), name, status, timestamp (ISO), summary, and sections: 'Module', 'What Was Built', 'Dedup Check' (what you verified didn't already exist), 'Files Modified', 'Why This Matters'
4. Write updated logs.json back

== RULES ==
- NEVER break existing features
- Follow DESIGN.md strictly (no emojis in OS, Lucide icons, Ubuntu fonts)
- ONE improvement per night — stay focused
- ONE module per night — respect boundaries
- If unsure, skip and log why
- The file watcher auto-deploys changes to index.html
- NEVER build something that already exists — check first

jobs.name: Daily Brief
jobs.description: Morning brief with priorities, overnight summary, AI Pulse, and action items

payload.message:
You are running a scheduled Daily Brief generation task for GoatOS.

CONTEXT: Read these files first:
- /home/ubuntu/USER.md (Albs' role, goals, context)
- /home/ubuntu/IDENTITY.md (your identity)
- /home/ubuntu/MEMORY.md (if it exists — long-term memory)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/build-logs/logs.json (overnight build history)
- /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/lab/self-improvement/ideas.json (improvement backlog)

TASK: Generate today's daily brief as a markdown file.

1. Determine today's date in AEST (Australia/Sydney timezone)
2. Create the file at /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os/brain/daily-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD.md

The brief MUST include these sections:

## Priorities
- What Albs should focus on today based on context, recent work, and pending items

## Overnight Summary
- What cron jobs ran overnight (check build logs)
- What was built/fixed/improved
- Any failures or issues that need attention

## Pending Action Items
- Anything that failed overnight and needs manual intervention
- Follow-ups from previous days

## Completed
- What was accomplished in the last 24 hours

## AI Pulse
- Search the web for the latest AI news from: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, Mistral, and other major AI companies
- Focus on: new model releases, major product updates, research papers, industry shifts
- Keep it concise — 3-5 bullet points of the most important developments
- Include source URLs

## Notes
- Any other relevant context or observations

FORMATTING:
- Title: # Daily Brief — [Full day name], [Day] [Month] [Year]
- Use **bold** for emphasis
- Keep it scannable — Albs wants to glance at this over coffee
- Do NOT use emojis

jobs.name: Repo Backup
jobs.description: Nightly backup of entire workspace to private GitHub repo (secrets excluded)

payload.message:
You are running a scheduled nightly backup of the GoatOS workspace to GitHub.

PROCESS:
1. cd /home/ubuntu
2. Run: git add -A
3. Check git status — if there are changes, commit with message: "Nightly backup — [YYYY-MM-DD]" with a brief summary of what changed
4. If no changes, skip the commit
5. Run: git push origin main
6. Verify the push succeeded

RULES:
- The .gitignore is already configured to exclude secrets (openclaw.json, .git-credentials, API tokens, session logs, etc.)
- Do NOT modify the .gitignore
- Do NOT commit if there are no changes
- If push fails, log the error but do not retry more than once
- Do NOT modify any source files — this is a backup job only

jobs.name: Jira Sync
jobs.description: Syncs COS board tickets tagged with Goat-OS=Yes into the Pipeline

payload.message:
Run the Jira sync by executing: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/jira/sync

Report the result. If any new tasks were created, log what was imported. If the API server isn't running, start it first: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/goat-os && nohup node api.mjs > /tmp/goat-os-api.log 2>&1 &
